user.title: Octopus EVSE IOG Manager — Global Settings

data.dry_run: Dry run mode (calculate only — do not write to Octopus)

data_description.registered_battery_kwh: The battery size Octopus has on record for your Intelligent Go tariff/EVSE. Used as the reference for scaling charge targets when a different vehicle is plugged in.
data_description.typical_max_charger_power_kw: Used to calculate the estimated charging time. Set to your charger's typical maximum output (default 7 kW).
data_description.plug_stabilisation_delay: How long to wait after a vehicle is detected as plugged in before reading its SoC and setting the charge target. Increase if your car integration is slow to report a fresh SoC.
data_description.dry_run: When enabled, the integration calculates the target % and logs it but does not write to the Octopus Energy integration. Use this to validate before going live.

data_description.vehicle_charging_loss_percent: Helps predict the total dispatch and estimate total charging time. Leave at 10% if you're not sure.
data_description.vehicle_rate_limit_soc_percent: If your car rate-limits near the end of charge, set the SoC where that starts (e.g. 95%). Helps estimate total charging time. Leave at 100% if you don't care about charging-time estimates.
data_description.vehicle_rate_limit_power_kw: Used only if the rate-limit SoC is below 100%. The reduced power the car pulls above the knee. Helps estimate total charging time.

add_another.title: Add Another Vehicle?
add_another.description: You have added {count} vehicle(s). Add another?

abort.already_configured: Octopus EVSE IOG Manager is already configured. Use the Options flow to make changes.

edit_vehicle.description: Editing {vehicle_name}. Review and set any new options, then submit.

recalculate.name: Recalculate
recalculate.description: Force an immediate recalculation of the IOG charge target for all vehicles currently locked in (target already set), resetting them to the waiting state.
